hero.badge: For Indian Travelers in Transit → UK Stopover
hero.title: Turn Your UK Stopover Into
hero.subtitle: Transiting through the UK on your way to the US or beyond? Don't sit at the airport — London is waiting for you.

yes: You are eligible for a UK stopover visa
Final destination: USA, Canada, Australia, etc.
You have 8 hours to 2 days here
no: The UK is your final destination
You're already on a UK visa
You have less than 6 hours stopover

visa.intro: Indian passport holders must check transit visa status before leaving India. Getting this wrong means being stuck at the airport.
visa.alertBody: Whether you need a visa depends on whether you plan to leave the airport. Staying airside (inside terminal) has different rules from going out to explore London.

cards.title: Want to Explore London?
cards.body: You must apply for a Standard Visitor Visa or Transit Visitor Visa before travel. UK Border Force will not let you exit without it. Apply at least 3–4 weeks in advance on the UK Visas & Immigration portal.

cards.title: Staying Inside the Airport?
cards.body: If you hold a valid US visa (B1/B2), Canadian, Australian, or Schengen visa, you may be exempt from the Direct Airside Transit Visa (DATV). Always verify on official UK gov website — rules change frequently.

cards.title: Applying for a Transit Visa
cards.body: Apply online at gov.uk/transit-visa. You need your passport, flight details, proof of onward travel (US visa + ticket), and a photo. Processing: 3–8 weeks. Fee: approx. £115.

cards.title: US Visa Holders — Extra Benefit
cards.body: Valid US B1/B2 visa holders with a confirmed onward US ticket may qualify for the Electronic Travel Authorisation (ETA) — faster and cheaper than a full visa. Check eligibility on gov.uk/eta.

visa.warning: 🔴 Always check gov.uk for the latest requirements. Vaaradhi Tours provides guidance only — we are not a visa service.

8h.note: Assumes flight lands ~8:00 AM and you're back at airport by 4:00 PM. Adjust proportionally. Always add 2 hr airport buffer.

steps.title: Land, Clear Immigration & Store Bags
steps.body: Arrive at Heathrow (LHR). Budget 60–90 mins for UK Border Control. Store bags at airport luggage storage (~£8–12/bag).
steps.tip: Eat Indian snacks now — customs may not allow them beyond the airport.

steps.title: Heathrow to Central London
steps.body: Heathrow Express → Paddington: 15 mins, every 15 mins (~£25). Or Elizabeth Line (Tube): 45 mins, ~£5.60. Buy an Oyster card at the airport.
steps.tip: Elizabeth Line is budget-friendly and scenic. Buy return tickets upfront.

steps.title: Hyde Park — Morning Stroll
steps.body: 10 mins from Paddington. Walk the Serpentine lake, Kensington Gardens. Free entry, beautiful in all weather.

steps.title: Westminster Highlights Walk
steps.body: Big Ben → Houses of Parliament → Westminster Bridge → Victoria Embankment → Buckingham Palace. All within 2 km. Iconic photos guaranteed.
steps.tip: Changing of the Guard at Buckingham Palace: 11:00 AM (check — not daily).

steps.title: Indian Lunch in Southall
steps.body: 30 mins from central London. Authentic Punjabi and South Indian food. Try Brilliant Restaurant or Gifto's Lahore Karahi. Budget: £10–15/person.

steps.title: Quick Shopping
steps.body: Oxford Street for high-street brands (Primark, M&S, H&M). Southall for Indian clothes, sweets, and travel snacks.

steps.title: Head Back to Heathrow
steps.body: Take Heathrow Express or Elizabeth Line back. Allow 2 hours before departure for international check-in and security.
steps.tip: Confirm which terminal your next flight departs from before leaving the city.

1d.note: A full day (9 AM – 9 PM) gives time for London's iconic sites plus an authentic Indian experience. Stay overnight if needed — budget hotels from £70/night.

steps.title: Arrive, Store Bags & Breakfast
steps.body: Store luggage at hotel or airport. Grab breakfast — Pret, Costa, or wait for Indian food in the city.

steps.title: South Bank & London Eye
steps.body: London Eye: 360° city view (book online: £33, 30-min ride). Walk along South Bank — Thames views are free and stunning.
steps.tip: Book London Eye tickets 24h in advance — skip the queue, save 20%.

steps.title: Tower of London & Tower Bridge
steps.body: Crown Jewels at the Tower (£34) or photograph Tower Bridge for free from outside. One of London's most iconic landmarks.

steps.title: Lunch at Brick Lane
steps.body: East London street lined with Indian & Bangladeshi restaurants. Great dal, biryani, karahi. Budget: £12–18. Try Aladin or Preem.

steps.title: British Museum — FREE
steps.body: One of the world's greatest museums — completely free. The South Asia gallery features Mughal art, Hindu sculpture, and artefacts from across the subcontinent.
steps.tip: A must-see for Indian visitors — don't miss the South Asia galleries.

steps.title: Covent Garden & Leicester Square
steps.body: Street performers at Covent Garden (free), Long Acre shops, Leicester Square. M&Ms World and LEGO Store for kids' gifts.

steps.title: Sunset — Primrose Hill
steps.body: Panoramic sunset view over London skyline — free, breathtaking, and uncrowded. Or pre-book Sky Garden (rooftop tropical garden, also free).

steps.title: Dinner in Southall or Wembley
steps.body: Gifto's Lahore Karahi (Southall) for Punjabi food, or Sakonis (Wembley) for vegetarian Gujarati food. Best Indian outside India.

2d.note: Two days is a luxury! Day 1 covers London classics. Day 2 goes deeper — a day trip outside London or exploring Indian communities.
2d.day1Note: Follow the full 1-Day plan above. Stay at: Travelodge (£70–90/night) or Premier Inn (£80–110/night) near Paddington or Victoria.

steps.title: Windsor Castle Day Trip
steps.body: Home of the British Royal Family. Train from Paddington to Windsor: ~£14 return, 50 mins. Walk Windsor town, see the castle, Long Walk. Entry: £30.

steps.title: Stonehenge & Bath Day Trip
steps.body: Organised coach tours from London: Stonehenge + Roman Baths in Bath. Book via Viator or GetYourGuide (£70–90 including transport). UNESCO World Heritage city.

steps.title: Southall & Wembley — Little India
steps.body: Southall Broadway: Punjabi shops, fresh samosas, Indian sweets, Bollywood. Wembley: Gujarati and South Indian food trail. Feels closest to home.

steps.title: V&A Museum — FREE India Galleries
steps.body: Victoria & Albert Museum has the world's finest Indian art collection outside India — Tipu Sultan's artefacts, Mughal textiles, Hindu deities. Unmissable.

steps.title: Final Shopping
steps.body: Harrods for luxury gifts. Primark for clothes. Marks & Spencer for food gifts. Boots for No7 skincare (very popular in India). Claim VAT refund at airport.

attractions.name: Tower of London
attractions.description: UNESCO site with Crown Jewels, Yeomen Warders, and 900 years of royal history. Fascinating connections to India's colonial past.

attractions.name: London Eye
attractions.description: 135m observation wheel on the Thames. Best at dusk for magical city lights. Book online in advance for savings and no queue.

attractions.name: British Museum
attractions.description: The South Asia gallery holds incredible artefacts from India — Amaravati sculptures, Mughal art. A must for Indian visitors.

attractions.name: Buckingham Palace
attractions.description: Changing of the Guard at 11:00 AM. Walk down The Mall for great photos of the King's official residence.

attractions.name: Hyde Park
attractions.description: Serpentine lake, Kensington Gardens, Diana Memorial Fountain. Perfect for an early morning walk or late evening wind-down.

attractions.name: V&A Museum
attractions.description: World's greatest decorative arts museum. India galleries feature Tipu Sultan's belongings, Mughal jewellery, and intricate textiles.

options.name: Heathrow Express
options.description: Fastest — 15 mins to Paddington. Every 15 mins from 5 AM. Clean and direct with no stops.

options.name: Elizabeth Line (Tube)
options.description: Best value. 45–60 mins to central London. Buy an Oyster card or use contactless. Available at airport.

options.name: National Express Coach
options.description: Cheapest but slowest — 60–90 mins to Victoria Coach Station. Book in advance for best prices.

options.name: Uber / Black Cab
options.description: Most convenient. Uber cheaper than cabs. Great for groups of 3+ splitting the fare. Uber accepts Indian payment methods.

tips.title: Oyster Card
tips.body: Tap in/out everywhere. Daily cap ~£15 (Zone 1–2) — you never overpay.

tips.title: Google Maps
tips.body: Download offline maps. Shows Tube + walking. Real-time disruption alerts.

tips.title: Citymapper App
tips.body: Best London navigation app. Exact walking routes, bus stops, live alerts. Free.

tips.title: Walk More
tips.body: Big Ben→London Eye: 5 min walk. Covent Garden→British Museum: 8 min. London is walkable!

food.name: Little India — Punjabi Heaven
food.body: Southall Broadway is the most authentic Indian neighbourhood. Fresh samosas, tandoori chicken, mithai shops. Try Brilliant Restaurant or Gifto's Lahore Karahi. £10–18/person.

food.name: Curry Capital of London
food.body: Famous street of Indian & Bangladeshi curry houses. Great dal, biryani, karahi. Staff invite you in from the pavement. Try Aladin or Preem. £12–20/person.

food.name: Gujarati & South Indian Food
food.body: Sakonis — legendary vegetarian Gujarati & South Indian (pani puri, dhokla, dosas). Great for vegetarians. Chetna's also excellent. £8–14/person.

food.name: South Indian & Sri Lankan
food.body: Apollo Banana Leaf for authentic Tamil food. Spice Village for Kerala dishes. Idli-sambar, chettinad curries available. £10–16/person.

food.name: Indian Sweets & Snacks
food.body: Southall mithai shops: gulab jamun, jalebi, barfi, halwa. Buy boxed sweets to carry to the US. Stock up on Haldiram's and MTR snacks for the journey.

food.name: Vegetarian-Friendly London
food.body: All Indian restaurants mark vegetarian dishes clearly. Jain-friendly options in Wembley and Harrow. Always ask about egg in sauces if strictly vegetarian.

essentials.title: Currency & Payments
items: Wise card for best INR→GBP rate

essentials.title: SIM Card & Internet
items: Buy Giffgaff or Three SIM at airport
Airalo eSIM — buy from India before travel

essentials.title: Plugs & Adapters
items: UK uses Type G plugs (3 square pins)

essentials.title: Safety & Emergency
items: UK is very safe for tourists

tips.title: Add 2 Hours Buffer
tips.body: UK immigration can take 60–90 mins. Always add 2 hr buffer on each end of your stopover — never assume a quick exit.

tips.title: Carry a Wise Card
tips.body: Best INR-to-GBP rate with no hidden fees. Load it from India before travel. Works at all UK ATMs and shops.

tips.title: Pre-Book Attractions
tips.body: London Eye and Tower of London have long queues. Book 24–48 hours online to save time and money — sometimes 20% off.

tips.title: Pack an Umbrella
tips.body: London rain is light but frequent. A small travel umbrella is essential. Don't let a shower ruin your sightseeing plans.

tips.title: Walk More, Tube Less
tips.body: Many attractions are walking distance from each other. The tube map looks huge but walking between stops is often faster.

tips.title: Download Offline Maps
tips.body: Download Google Maps for London offline before arriving. UK roaming on Indian SIMs is expensive. Use airport WiFi to download.

tips.title: Keep Documents Handy
tips.body: Keep onward flight ticket and visa easily accessible. UK Border Force may ask for proof of onwards travel at entry.

tips.title: Try British Experiences
tips.body: Ride a red double-decker bus, see a red phone box, have fish & chips. These small moments are uniquely British.

tips.title: US Customs Alert
tips.body: US Customs is strict — don't bring fruits, vegetables, or dairy from the UK into the USA. Declare all food items.

tips.title: Share Your Journey
tips.body: London is incredibly photogenic. Plan your must-have shots — Tower Bridge, Big Ben, red bus on Westminster Bridge.
